insert into `core_news_translations` (`locale`, `origin_id`, `title`, `content`, `create_user`, `updated_at`, `created_at`) values ('en', 196, 'Discover Trung Trang Cave and the historical secrets of Cat Ba.', '

Trung Trang Cave is the largest and most beautiful natural cave on the Jade Island, famous for its system of thousand-year-old stalactites with magical shapes. Not only a masterpiece of nature, this place also preserves important historical secrets of Cat Ba, as it once served as a strategic military base (the Navy Cave) during the resistance war against the United States to save the nation.

 

Where is Trung Trang Cave located and why should you visit?

 

When people think of traveling to Hai Phong, they usually think right away of turquoise beaches, but the journey of exploring Trung Trang Cave is truly the distinctive highlight. Located about 15 km northwest of Cat Ba town, this cave sits right in the Trung Trang valley, within the management area of Cat Ba National Park.

 

This is a through-mountain cave about 300 m long, passing through the heart of the mountain with pristine, majestic beauty. Visiting this place not only lets you admire its geological beauty but is also an opportunity to learn about the proud historical secrets of Cat Ba. The space inside the cave always maintains a cool, dry temperature, very suitable for experiential trips with family or friends.

 

 

Exploring Trung Trang Cave – A stalactite masterpiece in the heart of Cat Ba Island

 

Stepping inside, you will immediately be overwhelmed by the magical space. Trung Trang Cave in Cat Ba is dubbed a "stalactite treasure" thanks to the diversity in the shapes and colors of its stalactite formations. Under the shimmering lights, the rock formations look like artistic statues meticulously sculpted by nature's hand over millions of years.

 

The stalactite system and geological formation

 

Scientists highly value the geological significance when exploring Trung Trang Cave. This cave belongs to the karst terrain characteristic of the Lan Ha Bay and Cat Ba region.



    • Sacred-creature-shaped stalactites: You will come across rock formations that look exactly like dragons, phoenixes, or herds of lions paying homage.

 

    • A diamond stone forest: In some nooks of the cave, the rock crystals sparkle like diamonds when light shines on them, creating an extremely dazzling scene.

 

    • The sound of stone: When you gently tap on certain stone columns, you will hear clear, bell-like echoes—an interesting experience when exploring Trung Trang Cave.



The unique structure of a through-mountain cave

 

Unlike many other caves, Trung Trang Cave has a structure that is open at both ends. This keeps the air inside the cave constantly circulating, with no stuffy feeling. As you go deeper into the cave, you will feel the change in temperature, usually 3–5°C lower than outside, making this an ideal "heat-escape" spot in the summer on Cat Ba Island.

 

 

Historical secrets of Cat Ba: Trung Trang Cave and its role in the war

 

Behind the magnificent beauty of nature lie heroic stories of courage. One of the historical secrets of Cat Ba that few people know is the name "Navy Cave." During the fierce years of the war, this cave became an important military base, protecting the sovereignty of the nation's seas and islands.

 

The Navy Cave – A steel fortress underground

 

From 1964 to 1968, when the U.S. imperialists escalated their bombing of the North, Trung Trang Cave was chosen as the stationing place for a signals unit of the Navy Command. With its rugged terrain and spacious interior, the cave could hold hundreds of soldiers along with the modern military equipment of that time.



    • Defense system: The soldiers took advantage of the natural rock walls to build workrooms, food storage, and a medical room.

 

    • A natural protective layer: The cave's roof, dozens of meters of solid limestone thick, was a steel shield against the enemy's bombing raids.



The historical traces that remain

 

Today, when exploring Trung Trang Cave, visitors can still observe traces of a glorious era. These are the steps carved into the rock, the flat ground where communication equipment was once placed, and the stories of the deep bond between the army and the people in the Cat Ba island region. Learning about the historical secrets of Cat Ba here helps us appreciate even more the value of peace and the beauty of our homeland.

 

In addition, discover many more high-quality travel experiences at Vietnamtoure.com

 

 

A guide to exploring Trung Trang Cave in Cat Ba safely and fully

 

The ideal time to visit

 

Cat Ba is most beautiful from April to October. However, because Trung Trang Cave lies within the mountain, you can visit at any season of the year.



    • Summer: Escape the scorching heat outside and enjoy the natural coolness.

 

    • Autumn: The air is dry, convenient for climbing and exploring Trung Trang Cave.



What should you prepare when visiting the cave?



    • Clothing: You should wear comfortable, moisture-wicking clothing. Don't forget to bring a light jacket, as the temperature in the cave is quite low.

 

    • Footwear: Use sneakers or hiking shoes with good grip to avoid slipping on the limestone slabs.

 

    • Lighting equipment: Although the cave already has an LED lighting system, bringing a small flashlight will help you see the nooks and stalactite details more clearly when exploring Trung Trang Cave.

 

    • Drinking water: Bring a small bottle of water to replenish your energy while walking through the cave.



 

 

 

Experiences not to be missed at Trung Trang Cave in Cat Ba

 

This journey does not stop at sightseeing. To truly feel the soul of the cave and the historical secrets of Cat Ba, try taking part in the following activities:

 

Take artistic photos with the stalactites

 

With the help of colorful artificial lighting, Trung Trang Cave is the perfect backdrop for stunning, "wow"-worthy photos. Look for angles with large stone columns or sparkling stone walls to capture your memories.

 

Listen to the echoes from the past

 

Take a moment of stillness in the middle of the cave to feel the space. You will hear the drip-drip of water falling from the cave ceiling and the sound of wind weaving through the rock crevices. It feels as if time has stopped, taking you back to the bygone historical secrets of Cat Ba, where the navy soldiers once lived and fought.

 

Combine your visit with the Kim Giao forest

 

After exiting through the cave's other entrance, you will be led into the famous Kim Giao forest. This is a precious wood that was often used to make chopsticks for kings in the old days to test for poison. It's a "2-in-1" journey, both exploring Trung Trang Cave and learning about the characteristic flora of Cat Ba Island.

 

 

Frequently asked questions about exploring Trung Trang Cave

 

1. Does visiting Trung Trang Cave take a lot of time?

 

Usually, it takes about 45 minutes to 1 hour to walk all the way through and explore Trung Trang Cave. If you stop to take photos and read the information boards about the historical secrets of Cat Ba, the time may extend a bit longer.

 

2. Can children and the elderly take part?

 

Yes, but with some caution. The cave has a few sections with low ceilings where you must bend down, and the stone steps can be slippery. However, the path is generally quite flat and much easier than many other caves in Vietnam. This is a great educational activity about history and nature for young children in Cat Ba.

 

3. Do I need to hire a guide when exploring Trung Trang Cave?

 

To deeply understand the stalactite legends as well as the historical secrets of Cat Ba about the Navy Cave, having a local guide is highly recommended. They will tell you stories rarely mentioned in books and newspapers, making your trip even more meaningful.

 

4. What gifts should I buy after visiting Cat Ba?

 

After exploring Trung Trang Cave, you can look for Cat Ba wild honey or handmade products made from Kim Giao wood at the shops near the National Park. These are specialties that carry the distinctive flavor of this island region.

 

 

Important notes for protecting the heritage of Trung Trang Cave

 

We are guests of nature, so let's be civilized visitors when exploring Trung Trang Cave:



    • Do not touch the stalactites: Sweat and bacteria from human hands can halt the growth of the stalactites or cause them to become discolored.

 

    • Do not litter: Carry all your waste out of the cave to keep Cat Ba's environment green and clean.

 

    • Keep quiet: Avoid making too much noise that affects creatures such as the bats living in the cave.

 

    • Follow the regulations: Always stick to the marked trail to protect the cave's structure and ensure your own safety.



Protecting these historical secrets of Cat Ba and this natural beauty is the responsibility of every individual, ensuring that future generations still have the chance to explore Trung Trang Cave.

 

 

The importance of preserving the historical secrets of Cat Ba

 

Caves like Trung Trang Cave are not only tourist attractions but also living pages of history. Every rocky nook, every path within the cave is soaked with the sweat and blood of our forefathers. When we explore Trung Trang Cave, we do not just go to look at the rocks and the cave, but also to pay tribute.

 

The land of Cat Ba always holds surprises. We hope that through this article, you now have an overview and feel inspired to pack your backpack and experience for yourself the historical secrets of Cat Ba and the indescribable beauty of this cave.

 

LIEN MINH VIET International Trading Company Limited
